summary: The anteroposterior (AP) X-ray view is least useful for diagnosing spondylolisthesis because it fails to accurately depict or quantify vertebral slippage, unlike lateral views, CT, or MRI. Lateral X-rays provide precise measurement of slip severity via the Meyerding classification, while CT detects bone abnormalities and MRI evaluates neurological complications.
